headline: Cathie Wood's ARK Says AMD Chips 'Can Already Be More Performant Per Dollar' Than Nvidia
summary: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD) is positioning itself to challenge Nvidia Corp.‘s (NASDAQ:NVDA) long-standing dominance in the lucrative AI accelerator market. According to a fresh analysis released by Cathie Wood‘s ARK Invest, internal research reveals that AMD chips “can already be more performant than Nvidia chips” per dollar on select workloads, highlighting a massive shift in data center cost efficiency. The CPU Playbook Applied to GPUs ARK Invest’s Director of Research, AI & Cloud

headline: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.

content.title: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
content.summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.

headline: Price Prediction: Can Intel Double by 2030?

headline: XSD vs. SMH: Should Your Semiconductor ETF Be Equal-Weight or Cap-Weight?
summary: The SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (NYSEARCA:XSD) and the VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SMH) both offer exposure to semiconductors, yet they are two very different bets. XSD spreads chips money almost evenly across roughly 40 U.S. semiconductor names. SMH funnels most of its dollars into the 25 largest, tilting hard toward the AI mega-caps. Over the last ... headline: SK Hynix, Micron, Meta, Delta, Circle Internet, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Stocks were rising slightly Friday and many technology shares were taking a breather as  SK Hynix  made its U.S. trading debut.  American depositary receipts of  SK Hynix   began trading at $170 on the Nasdaq and at last check were at $175.26, up 18% from the initial public offering price of $149.  The South Korean memory chip maker is issuing 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.

content.title: SK Hynix, Micron, Meta, Delta, Circle Internet, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
content.summary: Stocks were rising slightly Friday and many technology shares were taking a breather as  SK Hynix  made its U.S. trading debut. The South Korean memory chip maker is issuing 177.9 million ADRs, with 10 ADRs representing one common share.

headline: Altera returns to growth as AI, robotics fuel demand, CEO says
summary: Altera, a maker of programmable chips spun out of Intel, is growing roughly 20% a year and more than ‌doubling operating income as it prepares for an eventual public listing, Chief Executive Raghib ‌Hussain told Reuters in an interview.  Altera became fully independent last September after Intel agreed to sell a 51% stake ​to Silver Lake for $4.46 billion in a transaction valuing Altera at $8.75 billion.  Intel retains a 49% stake.

content.title: Altera returns to growth as AI, robotics fuel demand, CEO says
content.summary: Altera, a maker of programmable chips spun out of Intel, is growing roughly 20% a year and more than ‌doubling operating income as it prepares for an eventual public listing, Chief Executive Raghib ‌Hussain told Reuters in an interview.
